fonds.institution: Whyte Museum of the Canadian Rockies
fonds.scope: Photography, glacier observations, correspondence, publications. Includes photographic sequences from 1897-1913 of Illecillewaet, Yoho, and Asulkan glaciers. Lantern slides, photographs, postcards, prints, negatives.
fonds.findings: Found 260 photographs dated 1906 in V653. Series V653/I/Y contains nitrate negatives from 1906-1909. Series V653/PA contains 114 prints (1897-1907) of mountain landscape, railway, and glacier views. Multiple series with Yoho Pass, Emerald Lake, and glacier monitoring materials.
fonds.notes: Vaux brothers were amateur glaciologists who documented Alpine Club of Canada camp at Yoho Pass July 8-16, 1906. Sister Mary Vaux assisted with hand-coloring of photographs. Collection establishes foundation for Canadian glacier monitoring through repeat photography methodology.
sample_catalogues: V653/PS-3 (hand-coloured postcard, 1900-1906, hand-coloured by Mary M. Vaux)
V653/PA-1 to PA-114 (prints, 1897-1907, glacier and mountain views)

fonds.institution: Academy of Natural Sciences of Drexel University (ANSP)
fonds.fonds_title: Vaux family collection (possibly in mineralogy or natural history)
fonds.scope: William S. Vaux Sr. (father) was notable mineralogist affiliated with ANSP
fonds.findings: ANSP website accessible but no direct Vaux collection catalog found online. Requires in-person or direct institutional contact.
fonds.notes: William S. Vaux Sr. (1811-1882) was ANSP-affiliated mineralogist. George Jr. and William Jr. may have donated materials or correspondence to ANSP.

fonds.findings: Website not directly accessible from this environment. Vaux family were Philadelphia Quakers but no specific collection verified.
fonds.notes: Should contact Haverford directly for family correspondence and papers.

fonds.findings: Website accessible but no searchable catalog found online. Vaux family were prominent Philadelphia Quakers.
fonds.notes: THE major Quaker archive in North America. Likely holds family correspondence, genealogies, and possibly expedition records. Requires direct contact.

fonds.findings: Website accessible but search restricted from this environment. APS likely holds scientific correspondence and publications relating to glacier research.
fonds.notes: George Jr. may have corresponded with APS members about glacier observations. Requires direct contact or institutional access.

fonds.scope: Glacier monitoring using Vaux photographic sequences
fonds.findings: Vaux 1906-1913 glacier photographs are used in Parks Canada heritage interpretation programs for repeat photography monitoring.
fonds.notes: The Vaux glacier repeat photography project is foundational to Canadian glacier monitoring research. Contact Parks Canada directly for heritage archives.
